companyNameJapanese: 東京応化工業株式会社
description: World's leading photoresist manufacturer (branded 'THMR', 'OFPR', 'TFR' series) with ~25% global share across all lithography nodes from g-line (436nm) through i-line (365nm), KrF (248nm), ArF dry/immersion (193nm), to EUV (13.5nm). Also supplies high-purity chemicals (thinners, developers, strippers, rinses), antireflection coatings, hardmask materials, adhesion promoters, directed self-assembly (DSA) materials, and thick-film resists for advanced packaging. Operates Equipment segment manufacturing semiconductor/display processing equipment. Revenue split ~100% Materials (electronic functional materials + high-purity chemicals). FY ending December. Founded 1940 in Kawasaki. ~1,996 employees. Taiwan (TSMC) is the single largest revenue source at 36% of sales, followed by China 21%, Korea 13%, and Japan domestic. 3-for-1 stock split on Jan 1, 2024. Advancing metal oxide resist (MOR) technology for EUV/High-NA EUV applications alongside JSR.

majorClients: TSMC (largest customer, ~36% of revenue via Taiwan sales; TOK won TSMC supplier award; clustering near JASM Kumamoto)
Samsung Electronics (Korea; served from TOK Advanced Materials Co., Ltd. / TOKAM in Incheon/Pyeongtaek)
SK hynix (Korea; memory photoresists and packaging materials)
Intel (won Intel Preferred Quality Supplier Award 2020)
majorSuppliers: Gun Ei Chemical (4229.T, CMP-0038) — novolac resins for g/i-line photoresists; TOK holds 1.87% equity stake in Gun Ei; relationship since late 1970s
Maruzen Petrochemical (4634.T, CMP-0035) — advanced photoresist polymers for KrF/ArF/EUV nodes
Inpria (invested in; metal oxide resist technology for EUV)
Toyo Gosei (4970.T) — photoacid generators (PAGs) used in chemically amplified resists
competitors: JSR Corporation (4185.T; taken private by JIP for $6.4B; TOK's closest rival with ~91% combined global share; advancing MOR for EUV)
Shin-Etsu Chemical (4063.T) — #2-3 globally; strong in ArF immersion resists
FUJIFILM Electronic Materials (4901.T) — broad photoresist portfolio
Merck / AZ Electronic Materials — acquired by Merck KGaA

FY2025_ended_Dec2025.note: Record highs for both revenue and operating income. Driven by generative AI demand and FX tailwinds.

FY2024_ended_Dec2024.note: Recovery driven by semiconductor market rebound and generative AI demand.

FY2023_ended_Dec2023.note: Down year due to semiconductor downcycle. Still profitable.

FY2026_forecast.note: Continued growth expected from advanced node migration and AI chip demand.

FY2025_Jan2026_revision.reason: Higher-than-expected generative AI demand; FX assumption revised from 135 to 150 yen/dollar

dividendHistory.FY2023: 168 yen pre-split / 56 yen post-split equivalent (DOE 3.8%, payout ratio 53.3%)
dividendHistory.policy: DOE target of 4.0% plus flexible share buybacks

shareBuybackHistory.latestBuyback: Up to 2.5M shares (2.05% of total), max 7B yen

capacityExpansion: Koriyama Plant (Fukushima, Japan): New manufacturing building, >20B yen investment, construction from Jul 2024, operations 2H 2026. Will be Japan's largest photoresist facility. Produces EUV/ArF/KrF photoresists.
Aso Kumamoto Site (Kikuchi City, Kumamoto, Japan): New site near TSMC JASM. Completed June 2024. Proximity to TSMC Kumamoto Fab 1 and Fab 2.
Pyeongtaek Plant (South Korea, via TOKAM): Second Korea site, 20B yen investment. Operations begin ~2030. Will 3-4x Korea production capacity. Serving Samsung and SK hynix.
Korea high-purity chemicals plant: Separate 12B yen investment for semiconductor chemicals.
Micro Resist Technology GmbH (Germany): Fully acquired Feb 2025. Strengthens European presence and optical/electronic materials R&D.

EUVphotoresistPosition.massProductionStatus: Approved and in use at mass production lines at leading fabs
EUVphotoresistPosition.marketShare: ~25% of EUV photoresist market (JSR est. ~30-35%, Shin-Etsu ~15-20%)
EUVphotoresistPosition.technologyDirection: Advancing metal oxide resist (MOR) for sub-2nm / High-NA EUV; invested in Inpria (MOR pioneer)
EUVphotoresistPosition.competitiveDynamic: TOK + JSR together ~91% of global photoresist market. Japanese firms (JSR, TOK, Shin-Etsu) supply >70% of advanced EUV-grade resists.
EUVphotoresistPosition.note: Key battleground is 2nm node and beyond. MOR delivers higher resolution than conventional chemically amplified resists (CAR). Chinese competitors limited to i-line/KrF; cannot yet produce EUV-grade resists.

keyRisks: Customer concentration: TSMC alone is ~36% of revenue; any demand fluctuation at TSMC directly impacts TOK
JSR privatization by JIP: With deep pockets and consolidated R&D, JSR may aggressively invest in EUV/MOR, intensifying competition
Technology transition risk: If MOR displaces conventional CAR-based EUV resists faster than expected, TOK's existing CAR EUV portfolio faces obsolescence
FX sensitivity: ~80%+ overseas revenue; 150 yen/dollar assumed in FY2025; yen appreciation would compress margins
China risk: 20.5% of revenue from China; geopolitical tensions, export controls could restrict sales of advanced materials
Korea localization pressure: Samsung/SK hynix may push for domestic Korean suppliers (Dongjin Semichem) reducing reliance on Japanese imports
Capex intensity: Major simultaneous investments in Koriyama, Kumamoto, and Pyeongtaek; execution risk on timing and returns
Cyclicality: Semiconductor materials demand is cyclical; FY2023 showed -7.5% revenue decline during downturn
Valuation: P/E of 33x and P/B of 5x are elevated vs historical averages; stock up 143% over past 12 months
growthDrivers: EUV adoption: Every advanced logic/memory node increases EUV photoresist consumption; ASML backlog supports multi-year growth
AI chip demand: Generative AI drives demand for cutting-edge logic chips (3nm, 2nm) requiring advanced photoresists
Advanced packaging: Thick-film resists for chiplet integration, fan-out wafer-level packaging, HBM stacking
TSMC expansion: JASM Kumamoto (Fab 1 operational, Fab 2 by 2027 at 6nm); TOK positioned as local supplier
High-NA EUV: Next-generation lithography (0.55 NA) will require entirely new resist chemistries; TOK investing in R&D
Geographic expansion: Korea capacity 3-4x increase by 2030; US production aligned with CHIPS Act incentives
Product mix improvement: Shift toward high-value-added advanced materials (EUV, ArF immersion) improving margins

ShinEtsu_4063T.note: Much larger diversified chemical; lower multiples

JSR_4185T.note: Taken private by JIP at ~$6.4B in 2024; no longer publicly traded

valuationContext.note: TOK trades at a premium to Shin-Etsu on P/E, reflecting its pure-play photoresist growth profile and higher growth rate. Premium appears justified by 18-20% revenue growth and 43%+ operating income growth in FY2025, but 52-week range of 2,519-9,410 yen shows significant recent re-rating.

_externalRelationships.stockSplitNote: 3-for-1 stock split effective January 1, 2024. All per-share figures after this date are on post-split basis.

analystConsensus.note: As of Feb 2026. Wide range reflects uncertainty on cycle sustainability.

_externalRelationships.notes: TOK is the world's preeminent photoresist company and a critical chokepoint in the global semiconductor supply chain. Together with JSR, the two Japanese firms control ~91% of the global photoresist market. TOK's vertical integration strategy (from monomers to finished resists, tailored to each fab's process) creates deep customer lock-in ('suriawase'). The company was founded in 1940, has headquarters in Kawasaki, and operates plants across Japan (Koriyama, Gotemba, Utsunomiya, Aso/Kumamoto), Korea (Incheon, Pyeongtaek), Taiwan, and the US (Oregon). Recent 143% stock price appreciation in 12 months reflects the market's recognition of TOK's central role in the AI semiconductor boom. Key watchpoint: MOR technology adoption at 2nm and High-NA EUV could reshape competitive dynamics.

_metadata.dataSource: Company IR (tok.co.jp), Yahoo Finance, TrendForce, DIGITIMES, Nasdaq, TipRanks, Globe and Mail, MarketScreener, StockAnalysis, Morningstar, CNBC, Bloomberg, DCFModeling, Mordor Intelligence, TradingView
_metadata.notes: Deep research update 2026-02-23. FY2025 record results: revenue 237.0B (+17.9%), OP 47.3B (+43.2%), OPM 20.0%. Stock up 143% over 12 months; P/E 33.2x, P/B 5.07x. Foreign ownership 26.58%. Taiwan/TSMC is 36% of revenue. Koriyama expansion (>20B yen, 2H 2026), Pyeongtaek Korea plant (20B yen, ~2030). Micro Resist Technology GmbH acquired Feb 2025. EUV photoresist ~25% global share. Active buyback program.
